input.background: A customer-experience software company conducted a nationwide survey of 1,859 U.S. adults who had conducted at least one online transaction at a banking, shopping, travel, or insurance website in the past year. The survey aimed to understand consumer feelings about using the Internet for online shopping. The findings revealed that 1,655 respondents (89%) experienced technical problems with an online transaction, and more than one-third of consumers go to a competitor's website when a glitch occurs.
input.question: Identify the target population.

output.answer: Presumably, the software company who commissioned the survey is interested in all consumers who have made at least one online transaction in the past year. Consequently, the target population is all consumers who use the Internet for online transactions.

meta_info.caption: Method of Data Collection-Survey of Online Shoppers
